original: Hurricane hits villages in Holland/Friesland, causing floods
Mongolian forces take the Russian city of Ryazan after a five-day siege during their invasion of the Rus
Earthquake in England causes Wells' cathedral roof to collapse
Antoine Perrenot de Granvelle, Archbishop of Mechelen, made Cardinal
Battle of Curalaba: the Mapuche people led by Pelentaru revolt and inflict a major defeat on Spanish troops in southern Chile
Spanish Queirós Expedition departs Callao, Peru, led by Pedro Fernandes de Queirós in attempt to find Terra Australis (finds instead Espíritu Santo and other Vanuatu islands)
Hue Tay Son becomes emperor Quang Trung of Vietnam
First stone arch railroad bridge in the US is dedicated in Baltimore
HMS Beagle sails into Bay of Islands, New Zealand
The Rochdale Pioneers commence business at their cooperative in Rochdale, England, starting the cooperative movement
1st US skating club formed (Philadelphia)
Fetterman Massacre: Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho Indians kill all 81 US Army soldiers, the worst military disaster suffered by the U.S. Army on the Great Plains at the time
Pim Mulier 1st & only trip to "Alvesteddetocht"

British Parliament pass two important pieces of social legislation: The Trades Disputes Bill, legalizing peaceful picketing, and The Workingmen's Compensation Act, broadening employers' liability for accidents
Dutch government of De Master falls due to war budget
1st junior high school established (Berkeley California)
Unitversity of Copenhagen rejects American Explorer Frederick A Cook's claim that he was 1st to North Pole
Explosion in coal mine in Hulton England, 344 mine workers dies
First crossword puzzle (with 32 clues) printed in NY World
2,800 African miners strike at the Van Rhyn Deep mines in a bid to redress some of their grievances
10.17" (25.83 cm) of rainfall, Glenora, Oregon (state record until 2006)
Red Sox trade Dutch Leonard, Ernie Shore & Duffy Lewis to Yankees for Ray Caldwell & Slim Love, Frank Gilhooey, Al Walters & $15,000
Supreme Court rules Arizona statute protecting labor rights to picket and insulating disputes from court injunctions is unconstitutional
Nepal changes from British protectorate to independent nation
"Battleship Potemkin", Soviet silent film directed by Sergei Eisenstein, starring Aleksandr Antonov, Vladimir Barksy and Grigori Aleksandrov, premieres in Moscow
Test batsman Hunter "Stork" Hendry scores career best unbeaten 325 for Victoria against New Zealand in drawn tour match at the Melbourne Cricket Ground
1st group hospital insurance plan offered (Dallas Tx)
Dried human blood serum 1st prepared, University of Pennsylvania
Newfoundland reverts to being a crown colony of Great Britain

Chicago Black Hawks left wing Paul Thompson becomes first player in NHL history to score against his brother, Boston Bruins goaltender Cecil Thompson; scores with just 9 seconds left in regulation as Bruins win 2-1
Chicago Bears Ray McLean makes last NFL drop kick for an extra point
David Diamond's 1st Symphony premieres  with the New York Philharmonic-Symphony Society conducted by Dimitri Mitropoulos
German submarine U-567 sinks
US Supreme court declares Nevada separation legal
Cards' Marty Marion wins NL MVP
Earthquake in South Japan, kills 1,086
1949 NFL Draft: University of Pennsylvania linebacker/center Chuck Bednarik is selected first by Philadelphia Eagles
O'Neil Place in the Bronx erroneously renamed O'Neill Place
State of Eire (formerly Irish Free State) declares its independence
Dutch 1st Chamber accept sovereignty of Indonesia
Pioneering telenovela "Sua Vida Me Pertence" premieres in Brazil
Broadway Tunnel opens in San Francisco
WSBT TV channel 22 in South Bend, IN (CBS) begins broadcasting
KOMU TV channel 8 in Columbia, MO (NBC/PBS) begins broadcasting
Indonesia proclaims end to state of war
10th largest snowfall in NYC history (13.7")
Citizens of Deerfield Ill block building of interracial housing
Angolin leaves Comecon
US & Cuba negotiate accord for Cuba to release "Bay of Pigs" captives in exchange for $23M worth of medicine and baby food
USSR launches Luna 13; soft-landed in Oceanus Procellarum
David Crosby, Stephen Stills and Graham Nash (Crosby, Stills & Nash) premiere together in California
Vince Lombardi (Washington Redskins) coaches his last football game, a 20-10 loss to the Cowboys in Dallas, for a 7-5-2 season and 105-35-6 career
Oregon v. Mitchell Supreme Court case was decided, lowering the minimum voting age in U.S. federal elections to 18. The voting age for state and local elections was left to states discretion
WUTV TV channel 29 in Buffalo, NY (IND) begins broadcasting
A publican is killed as he tried to remove a bomb from his pub, Northern Ireland
Soviet Union signs a separate peace with East Germany
Israel, Egypt, Syria, Jordan, US & USSR meet in Geneva to discuss the Arab-Israeli conflict
1st NY Jet to gain 1,000 yards rushing (John Riggins)
Buffalo Sabres set NHL record of 40 points beating Caps 14-2 scoring 5 goals vs Washington Caps in 4:57
Madagascar adopts constitution
20th Islander shut-out opponent-Billy Smith 3-0 vs Bruins
Patricia R Harris named secretary of HUD
UN General Assembly passes a resolution declaring 1979-Year of Child
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Kaikoura Lights incident: possible UFO sighting recorded and filmed off the coast of New Zealand's South Island
Garry Unger’s NHL record for consecutive games ends at 914, as his Atlanta Flames lose 5-1 to the St Louis Blues and he is benched by coach Al MacNeil; record eventually broken by Doug Jarvis (964)
New constitution for Zimbabwe agreed
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Harold Carmichael ends NFL streak of 127 consecutive game receptions
Cincinnati beats Bradley 75-73 in 7 OTs (NCAA record)
NCAA rules rescinded last 2-minute men's basketball free throw rule
Brigham Young University (BYU) beats Michigan, 24-17 in the Holiday Bowl at Jack Murphy Stadium, San Diego to remain undefeated and secure their first ever NCAA Division I-A football title
Islander Kelly Hrudy's 1st shut-out win-Whalers 1-0
USSR launches Vega 2 for fly-by of Halley's Come
ARCO Anchorage runs aground near Port Angeles, WA
Heart's "Heart" album goes #1
3 white NY teens convicted of manslaughter in death of a black man

Soyuz TM-4 launches 3 cosmonauts - Vladimir Titov, Musa Manarov, and Anatoli Levchenko - to space station Mir. Titov and Manarov stay in space for just under 366 days, setting a new spaceflight record
Drexel agrees guilt to security felonies, pays a $650 million fine
Vladimir Titov & Musa Manarov return to earth (a year) with Chretien
New Zealand is the first country to set a formal inflation target (0-2%) for how much prices should rise each year. Similar targets are subsequently adopted by most developed countries.
VP Quayle sends out 30,000 Xmas cards with word beacon spelled beakon
Steve & Mark Waugh complete 464* partnership for NSW v WA
95 share in Madrid Spain $1.3 billion lottery (#47996)
El Sayid Nosair acquitted of killing Meir Kahane
Soviet Union formally dissolves as 11 of 12 republics sign treaty forming the Commonwealth of Independent States
Dutch DC-10 in fire at landing on Faro Portugal, 56 die
"The Sign" single released in the US by Ace of Base (Billboard Song of the Year 1994)
Bomb goes off on #4 train on Fulton Street NYC
City of Bethlehem passes from Israeli to Palestinian control
Martina Ertl of Germany wins her 3rd giant slalom world cup
SF Giants announce plans to build a new stadium to open in 2000
Pakistan all out 67 to lose to Tasmania by an inning
Detroit Lions Barry Sanders is 3rd to run for 2,000 yards in a season
Detroit Lions linebacker Reggie Brown, knocked unconscious in game
Spanish Civil Guard intercepts a van loaded with 950 kg of explosives that Basque separatist group ETA intended to use to blow up Torre Picasso in Madrid
Civil Partnership Act comes into force in the UK, with singer Elton John and David Furnish one of the first couples to form a same-sex civil union
Puzzle Play airs on Network Ten, replacing In the Box
Schengen Agreement area expands to include nine European Union member states: Czech Republic, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Poland, Slovakia, and Slovenia at land and sea borders
39 people are killed in violent clashes in Kenya
RapperFlavor Flavopens Flavor Flav's Chicken & Ribs causal restaurant in Sterling Heights, Michigan - closes July 2013
The NHL announces a further cancellation of games until January 14 bringing the total number of cancelled games to 625
UN General Assembly votes 128 to 9 to denounce US decision to recognize Jerusalem as Israel's capital
Governor of California Gavin Newson says there are now no intensive care beds left in Southern California or the San Joaquin Valley

United States attorney general announces charges against Libyan Abu Agela Mas’ud Kheir Al-Marimi, accusing him of involvement in constructing the bomb that destroyed Pan Am 103 over Lockerbie, Scotland, on the 32nd anniversary of the disaster
US begins vaccinating for COVID-19 using the Moderna vaccine
Major breakthrough in development of nuclear fission energy when experiment produces 59 megajoules of energy over five seconds at JET Laboratory, UK (reported Feb 2022)
Johan de Wittinstalled as Dutch Pensionary of Dordrecht
John Jaybecomes acting US Secretary of State (1789-90)
US Civil War:General Sherman's Union Army captures Savannah, Georgia, which surrenders to him, at the end of his March To the Sea Campaign
Phileas Fogg completes his round the world trip in 80 days, inJules Verne's "Around the World in Eighty Days"
First game of basketball, based on rules created byJames Naismith, played by 18 students in Springfield, Massachusetts. Celebrated today as world basketball day.
Mackenzie Bowellbecomes the 5th Prime Minister of Canada
Scientists Pierre andMarie Curiediscover radium
Gerhart Hauptmann's play "Michael Kramer" premieres in Berlin
Clyde Fitch's play "The City" premieres in NYC
1st feature-length silent film comedy "Tillie's Punctured Romance" released starringMarie Dressler, Mabel Normand andCharlie Chaplin
J. Edgar Hooverpersuades US to deport 250 alien radicals, including anarchist and feministEmma Goldmanand her husband to Russia
Jerome Kernand Buddy DeSylva's musical "Sally" premieres in NYC
Coco the Clownfirst appears for Bertram Mills Circus in Manchester, England
Fox Films signsShirley Templeaged 5, to a studio contract
French film "Zouzou" premieres in Paris, starringJosephine Baker; 1st black woman to star in a major motion picture
"Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs", the first full-length animated feature film and the earliest in theWalt DisneyAnimated Classics series, premieres at the Carthay Circle Theatre, Los Angeles
Adolf HitlernamesAdolf Eichmannleader of "Referat IV B", responsible for evictions and Jewish immigration
Morton Gould's "Minstrel Show" premieres in Indianapolis
"Samson and Delilah", directed and produced byCecil B. DeMille, starringHedy Lamarrand Victor Mature, premieres in New York
Montgomery bus boycott ends:Martin Luther King Jr.,Ralph Abernathy, E. D. Nixon, and Glenn Smiley ride in new integrated bus after Supreme Court rules segregated buses unconstitutional
Charles De Gaullewins 7 year term as 1st President of the 5th Republic of France
Tom Landryaccepts coaching job with Dallas Cowboys (stays until 1988)
Ornette Colemanrecords his influential album "Free Jazz: A Collective Improvisation", establishes the name for the Free Jazz sound
GangsterJoe Gallois sentenced to 7 to 14 years in state prison for extortion
US PresidentJohn F. Kennedyand British Prime MinisterHarold Macmillanmeet in Bermuda

French-Italian comic-drama film "King of Hearts" (Le Roi de cœur) directed byPhilippe de Broca, and starring Alan Bates premieres in France; achieves cult film status when it reaches US art houses in 1973
First manned Moon voyage launched withFrank Borman,Jim Lovelland William Anders aboard Apollo 8
Glen Campbell's album "Wichita Lineman" goes to #1 in the US
Diana Ross' final TV appearance as a Supreme on "TheEd SullivanShow", performing “Someday We’ll Be Together", after which Sullivan announced her upcoming departure from the group
UN Security Council choosesKurt Waldheimas 4th Secretary-General
Police in Des Plaines, Illinois, arrestJohn Wayne Gacyfor murder
Taiwanese-American AIDS researcherDavid Hois named Time Magazine's Man of the Year
Ballon d'Or: FC Barcelona's Brazilian midfielder Rivaldo is named best football player in Europe ahead of Manchester United midfielderDavid Beckhamand Milan striker Andriy Shevchenko
Joe Sakicis named captain of the 2006 Team Canada Olympic team
"Gangnam Style" byPsybecomes the first video to reach one billion views on YouTube
Carl Icahnis announced as Special Advisor to the President on Regulatory Reform, under PresidentDonald Trump
Indian sports drama film "Dangal" directed by Nitesh Tiwari and starringAamir Khan, Fatima Sana Shaikh and Sanya Malhotra premieres - first Indian film to earn $300 million worldwide
Hawaiian vocalist Israel Kamakawiwoʻole's official music video for his rendition ofHarold Arlenand Yip Harburg's "Over the Rainbow" reaches a billion views on YouTube
US President-electJoe Bidenreceives the Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine live on TV
Ukraine President VolodymyrZelenskyyvisits Washington, DC, meets with US PresidentJoe Bidenat the White House, and addresses Congress
Caitlin Clarkties Elena Delle Donne as the fastest Division I women's player to reach 2,000 career points in a game for Iowa against Dartmouth
Flanders adopts the Gregorian calendar, skipping 11 days making the next day Jan 1, 1583

Mayflower Pilgrims come ashore in Plymouth Bay, traditionally thought to be at Plymouth Rock, Massachusetts [OS: Dec 11] [some sources disagree, and OS/NS adds to confusion, could be +/- several days]
Elvis Presleymeets US President Richard Nixon in the White House - the image of this meeting is the most requested photo from the entire National Archives
A terrorist bomb destroys Pan Am Flight 103 mid-air over Scotland, killing all 259 passengers and crew on board and 11 people on the ground
The Mesoamerican Long Count calendar reaches the date 13.0.0.0.0
